result:
There is a library at the very edge of the universe, situated on a cliff of floating basalt where the fabric of space frays into silver threads. It doesn’t hold books in the traditional sense. Instead, its towering shelves are lined with glass jars of varying sizes, each containing a single breath of wind from a different world. 

Some jars hum with a low, resonant frequency, holding the warm breath of a dying star. Others rattle their lids, carrying the howling gales of a planet made entirely of shattered glass. The librarian is a creature of quiet geometry, shifting forms to dust the high shelves with appendages made of pure starlight. They know every jar by heart, cataloging the zephyrs and the hurricanes with equal care. 

Visitors are incredibly rare, mostly lost travelers or wandering comets seeking a moment of stillness. When they arrive, the librarian allows them to open exactly one jar. The wind escapes, whispering secrets of its home world into the visitor’s ear—a lullaby of crimson rains, the rustle of crystalline forests, the roar of obsidian oceans. 

Once the breath is spent, it doesn't return to the jar. It mingles with the void, becoming part of the cosmic wind that pushes galaxies along their endless, slow dance. The empty jar is then placed on a lower shelf, a quiet testament to a story briefly remembered and then released back into the infinite.
